data.title: Sobi Capital Markets Day 2026 aims to reach SEK 55bn revenue by 2030, bringing new ideas to life.
data.description: Sobi® (STO: SOBI) is currently holding its Capital Markets Day in Stockholm on February 18, 2026, with senior management sharing insights on the company's strategy. The focus is on achieving a revenue target of SEK 55bn by 2030. This initiative is part of building a strong and enduring foundation for the company's future growth.

data.title: Ether bulls aim for $2.5K on the back of staking ETF launch, RWA growth, showing increasing investor interest.
data.description: An increasing number of major endowments are embracing Ether, with a significant shift in capital towards the cryptocurrency. BlackRock has recently introduced a staking exchange-traded fund (ETF) for Ether. Additionally, Ethereum's stronghold on real-world assets is becoming more prominent, capturing the interest of traditional finance (TradFi) investors. This trend underscores the growing popularity and acceptance of Ether in the financial landscape.

data.title: BioArctic had a transformative year with record financial results. Q4 2025 saw launch of Leqembi Iqlik, with net revenues at SEK 184.0M, operating profit SEK 33.2M, and cash flow from operating activities at SEK 313.3M. CEO stated a record operating profit of over SEK 1.2 billion for 2025, with a successful year in terms of financial results, partnerships, and new projects. Leqembi now approved in over 50 markets, with growing sales. US, Japan, and China seeing steady increase in patients on treatment, ~350.3M M at the end of the period. Operations are progressing well in respective regions with revenue growth across markets, despite challenges faced in some areas like price reduction in Japan and inventory build-up in China. Read more on Benzinga.com.
data.description: BioArctic had a transformative year in 2025, achieving record financial results. During Q4, Leqembi was launched for maintenance dosing, approved in Brazil, Canada, and the UK, and new data presented suggested potential to delay disease progression by up to 8.3 years. Financially, net revenues were SEK 184.0M, operating profit was SEK 33.2M, and profit for the period was SEK -8.8M. Cash flow was strong at SEK 313.3M, with cash equivalents at SEK 2,190.4M. The CEO reported a fantastic year, with a dividend of 2.00 SEK per share proposed. Leqembi is now approved in over 50 markets, with increasing patient numbers in the US, Japan, and China. Despite currency impacts, royalty revenues grew by 31% in Q4. Eisai is progressing towards their full-year sales forecast for Leqembi.

data.title: Casino Group released new data showing a 15% increase in revenue, reaching $20 million in the last quarter.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.5%. This marks a significant decrease from the previous rate of 4% and represents the lowest level in over a decade. The report also highlighted a notable increase in job creation, with over 250,000 new jobs added last month alone. The strong job market has been attributed to growth in various sectors, including technology, healthcare, and manufacturing. Economists are optimistic about the future outlook, predicting continued growth and a further decrease in the unemployment rate in the coming months.

data.title: ICL's Q4 2025 sales at $1,701M, up 6% from Q4 2024, with operating income at ($16M) and adjusted income at $223M.
data.description: An international specialty minerals company, ICL, has released its financial report for the fourth quarter and full year ending on December 31, 2025. In the fourth quarter, they recorded consolidated sales of $1,701 million, marking a 6% increase from $1,601 million in the same quarter in 2024. Operating income saw a decrease to ($16) million compared to $147 million previously, but adjusted operating income rose to $223 million, a 17% increase from $190 million in 2024.

data.title: U.S. stock futures up; Analog Devices expects $2.31 per share on $3.12 billion revenue; Toll Brothers beat estimates.
data.description: US stock futures are up today, with Wall Street focusing on certain stocks: - Investors are eyeing Analog Devices Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI), expecting quarterly earnings of $2.31 per share on revenue of $3.12 billion. - After-hours trading saw Analog Devices shares increase by 0.7% to $340.01. - Toll Brothers Inc. (NYSE: TOL) released positive earnings for the first quarter, surpassing analyst estimates with quarterly earnings of $2.19 per share. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: IPUT Real Estate in Dublin raises €175 million from CBRE IM for office portfolio growth; holds 30 assets totaling 2M sq. ft.
data.description: Dublin's premier property investment firm, IPUT Real Estate, has secured a significant boost by raising €175 million in fresh equity from CBRE Investment Management Indirect Strategies. This capital injection will be utilized to further enhance IPUT's prestigious Dublin office portfolio, which currently encompasses 30 properties spanning over 2 million square feet exclusively situated in Dublin's City Centre. IPUT also boasts a robust development pipeline capable of supporting investments exceeding €500 million in value.

data.title: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong says Wall Street misunderstands crypto. Traditional finance is resistant to disruption. Armstrong warns laggards will fall behind. Coinbase (NASDAQ: COIN) is underappreciated.
data.description: Coinbase Global Inc.'s CEO Brian Armstrong acknowledged the company's misunderstanding on Wall Street, attributing it to traditional finance players' skepticism towards cryptocurrency disruption. Armstrong highlighted that while some have embraced crypto, others resist due to their careers being tied to the traditional financial system and viewing crypto as a threat. He compared this skepticism to taxi companies' disdain for Uber, noting that Wall Street's lag in understanding crypto could leave them behind. This underscores the divide in perceptions towards crypto within the financial sector. Armstrong's remarks were shared in an X post, and the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Aitech and Teledyne e2v collaborate to integrate advanced space computing capabilities into radiation-tolerant SP1 SBC for space missions.
data.description: Aitech and Teledyne e2v Semiconductors are collaborating to enhance space-grade computing, with Teledyne e2v’s reliable semiconductors integrated into Aitech’s SP1. The SP1 is a radiation-tolerant 3U SpaceVPX single-board computer optimized for various missions in space, such as LEO, GEO, lunar, and deep space. This collaboration aims to provide a robust computing platform for spacecraft, highlighting their commitment to advancing space technology.

data.title: 'The Actor's Algorithm' revolutionizes acting training with neuroscience. David Ihrig replaces old methods in Irvine, Calif. Feb 18, 2026 - PRNewswire.
data.description: David Ihrig has introduced a brain-based approach to enhance authentic performances, departing from traditional acting methods. Based in Irvine, California, as of February 18, 2026, this innovative technique replaces outdated practices that have been in use for over a century. The new system aims to provide actors with a more contemporary and effective means of honing their craft, moving away from techniques originating in late 19th-century psychology.

data.title: Deutsche Messe AG expands to Africa with Industrial Transformation Africa 2026 & Smart Factory Academy in Casablanca.
data.description: Deutsche Messe AG has extended its industrial transformation initiative to Africa by introducing Industrial Transformation Africa 2026 and launching the Smart Factory Academy in Casablanca. The event was announced in Dubai, UAE on February 18, 2026. This expansion will provide opportunities for businesses in Africa to engage with the latest technologies and trends in the industry. The platform aims to promote innovation and digitization in the industrial sector. The development signifies a major milestone for Deutsche Messe AG's global outreach, with anticipated participation from numerous companies and experts.

data.title: FSS completes major cloud transformation initiative in the Middle East, ensuring scalable and sovereign infrastructure.
data.description: In Mumbai, India, on February 18, 2026, Financial Software & Systems (FSS), a prominent player in payment technology globally, revealed the accomplishment of a significant cloud transformation project in the Middle East region. This initiative involved migrating vital customer platforms to the cloud, enhancing operational efficiency and scalability.

data.title: Alpha FMC and MDOTM Ltd announce whitepaper on AI-driven model portfolio management for asset managers, ensuring consistency and scalability.
data.description: Alpha FMC and MDOTM Ltd have jointly released a whitepaper titled "The Modern Model Portfolio," presenting an AI-driven model delivery system tailored for asset and wealth managers. With a focus on ensuring alignment with investment perspectives and facilitating scalable portfolio distribution, the innovative solution marks a significant step in modernizing industry practices. The collaboration between the two firms represents a key development in the financial sector, offering cutting-edge technology to enhance operational efficiency. This initiative, revealed in New York and London on Feb. 18, 2026, is poised to revolutionize the management of assets and wealth, with a keen emphasis on consistency and scalability.
